project_summary: This project aims to comprehensively analyze the evolution of car prices over time by integrating historical sales data, economic indicators, technological advancements, and consumer demand signals. The goal is to understand the key drivers behind price changes, including inflation, technology adoption, and shifting consumer preferences, ultimately developing predictive capabilities for future pricing trends to inform manufacturers, policymakers, and consumers.

sprints.sprint_objective: Establish the foundational environment and initial data strategy for the project.

mini_sprints.description: Set up the development environment, including programming languages (Python/R), necessary libraries (pandas, scikit-learn), version control, and project repository.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Configured development environment and initialized Git repository.

mini_sprints.description: Identify and document potential sources for all required datasets, including historical vehicle sales, economic, technology, and consumer demand data.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Document listing identified data sources and access strategies.

mini_sprints.description: Develop a conceptual data schema outlining the structure and relationships of the various datasets needed for analysis.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Conceptual data model diagram and initial data dictionary.

sprints.sprint_objective: Acquire and initially ingest all specified datasets into a structured storage system.

mini_sprints.description: Acquire and load historical vehicle sales data, including model, MSRP, sales volume, and key specifications, into the database system.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Raw historical vehicle sales data loaded into the database.

mini_sprints.description: Acquire and load inflation metrics (CPI) and other relevant economic indicators into the database.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Raw economic and inflation data loaded into the database.

mini_sprints.description: Acquire and load data related to technological advancements and consumer demand trends into the database.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Raw technology adoption and consumer demand data loaded into the database.

mini_sprints.description: Configure the chosen database system (e.g., SQL) for efficient storage and querying of all ingested raw data.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Configured database with initial raw data tables.

sprints.sprint_objective: Clean, standardize, and transform all raw data into an analysis-ready format.

mini_sprints.description: Clean and standardize vehicle sales data, handling missing values, correcting inconsistencies, and normalizing categorical features.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Cleaned and standardized vehicle sales dataset.

mini_sprints.description: Clean and preprocess economic and technological advancement data, ensuring consistent time alignment and data types.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Cleaned and aligned economic and technology datasets.

mini_sprints.description: Integrate all cleaned datasets into a unified analytical dataset, performing necessary joins and creating initial derived features.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Unified, analysis-ready dataset with initial engineered features.

sprints.sprint_objective: Conduct initial exploratory data analysis to understand data distributions, identify patterns, and establish a baseline understanding of price dynamics.

mini_sprints.description: Calculate and summarize key descriptive statistics for all relevant variables, including price, sales volume, and economic indicators.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Summary statistics report and initial data profiles.

mini_sprints.description: Generate initial visualizations (e.g., line charts) to observe overall car price trends and inflation over time.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Basic time series plots of nominal prices and inflation.

mini_sprints.description: Explore price distributions and trends across different vehicle segments (e.g., SUV, sedan, EV) using box plots and bar charts.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Visualizations comparing price characteristics across vehicle segments.

sprints.sprint_objective: Apply advanced statistical methods to quantify the impact of inflation, technology, and demand on car prices.

mini_sprints.description: Perform time series analysis and simple regression to quantify the direct impact of inflation on real car prices over time.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Analysis report on inflation-adjusted price trends and correlation.

mini_sprints.description: Use regression models to assess how the introduction and adoption rates of key technologies contribute to price changes.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Statistical analysis of technology's contribution to vehicle prices.

mini_sprints.description: Analyze how shifts in consumer preferences and segment-level demand trends affect average prices within and across vehicle categories.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Report on demand-side influences and segment-specific price dynamics.

mini_sprints.description: Develop multiple regression models to identify and quantify the combined influence of various factors (inflation, technology, demand, features) on vehicle prices.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Regression model results identifying key price drivers and their statistical significance.

sprints.sprint_objective: Develop and evaluate machine learning models for forecasting future car prices based on identified drivers.

mini_sprints.description: Refine feature selection and prepare data for predictive modeling, including handling categorical variables and scaling numerical features.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Prepared dataset optimized for machine learning models.

mini_sprints.description: Develop and train various regression models (e.g., Linear Regression, Ridge, Lasso) to predict car prices.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Trained and evaluated baseline regression models.

mini_sprints.description: Explore and implement more advanced machine learning models (e.g., Random Forest, Gradient Boosting) for price prediction.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Trained and evaluated advanced machine learning models.

mini_sprints.description: Evaluate model performance using appropriate metrics (e.g., R-squared, MAE, RMSE) and select the best-performing model(s) for forecasting.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Comparative model performance report and selected predictive model(s).

sprints.sprint_objective: Generate comprehensive reports and interactive visualizations to effectively communicate project findings to stakeholders.

mini_sprints.description: Draft the core data analysis report, including executive summary, methodology, key findings, and conclusions.
mini_sprints.deliverable: First draft of the comprehensive data analysis report.

mini_sprints.description: Create high-quality static visualizations (line charts, bar plots, scatter plots) to illustrate trends, correlations, and model results.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Set of static data visualizations for the report.

mini_sprints.description: Develop a prototype for an interactive dashboard (using Tableau/Power BI or Python/R libraries) to allow dynamic data exploration.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Interactive dashboard prototype (optional, based on time/tool availability).

sprints.sprint_objective: Finalize all project deliverables, incorporate stakeholder feedback, and ensure the project meets its success metrics.

mini_sprints.description: Gather feedback from stakeholders on the draft report and visualizations, and iterate on deliverables based on their input.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Revised data analysis report and visualizations incorporating feedback.

mini_sprints.description: Complete all project documentation, including data definitions, analytical decisions, model details, and future research recommendations.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Finalized project documentation and README.

mini_sprints.description: Review the project against defined success metrics (accuracy, interpretability, actionability) to ensure all objectives are met.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Validation report confirming project success metrics.
